## Introduction

The goal of this analysis is to evaluate multivariate normality using two approaches:

1. An approximate method based on Mardia’s skewness and kurtosis.
2. The Doornik–Hansen test, which provides a more reliable assessment.

This comparison allows us to understand the importance of proper calibration in statistical tests.

## Data generation

We simulate a dataset from a multivariate normal distribution with a predefined mean vector and covariance matrix.

```{R}
X <- as.data.frame(trees)

library(MVN)

result <- mvn(data = X, mvn_test = "doornik_hansen")

result$multivariate_normality
```
